en: Look, I wouldn't have come if it wasn't important.
ko: 보세요, 중요하지 않았다면 오지 않았을 거예요.

en: Okay. What is it?
Well, I went ahead and met with your Mr. Beneke,
which I will say again, loudly and for the record, bad idea.
Yes, but it is better than prison, correct?
Marginally, which is why I disbursed the funds as you requested.
ko: 그나마요, 그래서 요청하신 대로 자금을 분배했어요.

en: All right. Good.
I can't believe you actually bought it. Great Aunt Burgess.
ko: 정말로 그걸 샀다니 믿을 수가 없어요. 버지스 고모님.

en: What?
But you called it.
I mean, I guess people see those zeros dance before their eyes.
ko: 사람들이 그 제로들이 눈앞에서 춤추는 걸 보니까요.

en: It's kind of like highway hypnosis.
All right, so what's the problem?
Well, let's just say you and I don't wear the same rose-colored glasses
ko: 음, 당신과 저는 조니 패뷸러스에 대해 같은 장미빛 안경을 쓰고 있지 않아요.

en: where Johnny Fabulous is concerned,
ko: 그래서 그의 은행 계좌를 주시하고 신용 보고서를 확인했어요.

en: so I kept an eye on his bank accounts and I pulled his credit report.
If you would, note the time on that.
3.54 p.m. yesterday.
Three hours after leaving my office.
He bought a Mercedes SL550.
He's actually leasing it.
